title: Longest Ones After Flips
description:
Given a binary array and an integer k, return the length of the longest subarray containing at most k zeros. Think of flipping up to k zeros to ones to maximize consecutive ones.

    

Constraints:
    - 1 ≤ len(nums) ≤ 10^5
- nums contains only 0 or 1
- 0 ≤ k ≤ len(nums)
explanation:
Intuition
The window is valid if it contains at most k zeros. Expand right and count zeros. If the count exceeds k, move left to discard zeros until the window becomes valid again.

Approach
1. Initial Setup: Track the number of zeros in the window.
2. Main Algorithm: Advance right and increment zeros on 0. While zeros > k, move left and decrement when passing a 0.
3. Return Result: Record the longest valid window length.

Complexity Analysis
- Time Complexity: O(n) because the window grows and shrinks in a
  single pass without revisiting elements more than a constant number of
  times.
- Space Complexity: O(1) to maintain counts, sets, or running
  aggregates needed to validate the window.

Key Insights
- Counting constraint violations tells you when to shrink
- This pattern generalizes to at most K of some bad symbol

Common Pitfalls
- Not decrementing on leaving a zero keeps the window invalid
- Confusing exactly k with at most k
hints: Shrink only when zeros exceed k
